---
title: "Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells"
---

| 1534  | [exhaustion](#rune-1534)            | authored   | reviewed        | The resource is simply used up.                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

| 1542  | [inconsistent read](#rune-1542)     | authored   | reviewed        | A read that sees a world that never really existed as one coherent moment.                                                                                                                                                             |

| 1545  | [jitter spike](#rune-1545)          | authored   | generated_draft | `jitter spike` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the jitter spike obligation named before work proceeds.                   |
| 1546  | [leak {Curse}](#rune-1546)          | authored   | reviewed        | Something escapes the boundary that should have held it: memory, secrets, file descriptors, authority, abstraction, or time.                                                                                                           |
| 1547  | [livelock](#rune-1547)              | authored   | reviewed        | Everyone keeps moving but nobody makes progress.                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| 1548  | [lost update](#rune-1548)           | authored   | reviewed        | One writer quietly stomps another writer's valid change.                                                                                                                                                                               |
| 1549  | [memory corruption](#rune-1549)     | authored   | generated_draft | `memory corruption` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the memory corruption obligation named before work proceeds.         |

| 1552  | [missed heartbeat](#rune-1552)      | authored   | generated_draft | `missed heartbeat` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the missed heartbeat obligation named before work proceeds.           |
| 1553  | [null dereference](#rune-1553)      | authored   | generated_draft | `null dereference` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the null dereference obligation named before work proceeds.           |
| 1554  | [outage](#rune-1554)                | authored   | reviewed        | Service unavailable where availability was expected.                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| 1555  | [overflow](#rune-1555)              | authored   | reviewed        | Value exceeds the representable range.                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 1556  | [packet loss {Curse}](#rune-1556)   | authored   | generated_draft | `packet loss` in its Curse sense is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the packet loss obligation named before work proceeds.  |
| 1557  | [panic](#rune-1557)                 | authored   | generated_draft | `panic` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the panic obligation named before work proceeds.                                 |
| 1558  | [partial failure](#rune-1558)       | authored   | reviewed        | Some of the system failed while the rest kept limping.                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 1559  | [partition storm](#rune-1559)       | authored   | generated_draft | `partition storm` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the partition storm obligation named before work proceeds.             |
| 1560  | [poison pill](#rune-1560)           | authored   | generated_draft | `poison pill` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the poison pill obligation named before work proceeds.                     |
| 1561  | [race](#rune-1561)                  | authored   | reviewed        | An ordering curse: outcomes depend on timing you do not actually control. Name the race and the system becomes debuggable again.                                                                                                       |
| 1562  | [regression](#rune-1562)            | authored   | reviewed        | A previously solved bug or quality loss has returned.                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| 1563  | [resource starvation](#rune-1563)   | authored   | generated_draft | `resource starvation` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the resource starvation obligation named before work proceeds.     |
| 1564  | [retry storm](#rune-1564)           | authored   | reviewed        | Recovery logic amplifying failure instead of containing it. Unguided retries can turn a partial outage into a self-made siege.                                                                                                         |
| 1565  | [rollback failure](#rune-1565)      | authored   | generated_draft | `rollback failure` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the rollback failure obligation named before work proceeds.           |
| 1566  | [saturation](#rune-1566)            | authored   | generated_draft | `saturation` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the saturation obligation named before work proceeds.                       |
| 1567  | [schema drift](#rune-1567)          | authored   | reviewed        | The stored world and the assumed world have silently stopped matching. This is how migrations keep hurting after everyone thinks they are done.                                                                                        |
| 1568  | [split-brain {Curse}](#rune-1568)   | authored   | reviewed        | Separated leaders each believe they are authoritative.                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 1569  | [stack overflow](#rune-1569)        | authored   | generated_draft | `stack overflow` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the stack overflow obligation named before work proceeds.               |
| 1570  | [stale read](#rune-1570)            | authored   | generated_draft | `stale read` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the stale read obligation named before work proceeds.                       |

## Entries

### [1513] anomaly {#rune-1513}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Force:** `anomaly` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the anomaly obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `anomaly` at rune 1513 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `anomaly` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: `anomaly` is a failure analysis and counter-spells rune for name failure mechanisms without confusing symptom for cause; use it when the artifact needs the anomaly obligation named before work proceeds.
- Ask the assistant to state the `anomaly` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `anomaly` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

### [1517] cascading failure {#rune-1517}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** One failure fans out into many dependent failures.

**Shadow:** misusing `cascading failure` at rune 1517 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

### [1528] denial of service {#rune-1528}

**Status:** canonical

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

### [1546] leak {Curse} {#rune-1546}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Something escapes the boundary that should have held it: memory, secrets, file descriptors, authority, abstraction, or time.

**Shadow:** when treating a leak as the cause prevents finding the owner, lifetime, or boundary.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `leak` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Something escapes the boundary that should have held it: memory, secrets, file descriptors, authority, abstraction, or time.
- Ask the assistant to state the `leak` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `leak` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Something escapes the boundary that should have held it.

### [1547] livelock {#rune-1547}

**Pocket gloss:** Everyone keeps moving but nobody makes progress.

### [1561] race {#rune-1561}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** An ordering curse: outcomes depend on timing you do not actually control. Name the race and the system becomes debuggable again.

**Shadow:** when the race is patched locally while the shared state contract remains undefined.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `race` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: An ordering curse: outcomes depend on timing you do not actually control. Name the race and the system becomes debuggable again.
- Ask the assistant to state the `race` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `race` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Outcome depends on timing you do not actually control.

### [1562] regression {#rune-1562}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** A previously solved bug or quality loss has returned.

**Shadow:** misusing `regression` at rune 1562 in the failure lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `regression` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: A previously solved bug or quality loss has returned.
- Ask the assistant to state the `regression` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `regression` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** A previously solved bug or quality loss has returned.

### [1563] resource starvation {#rune-1563}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** generated_draft

**Example clause:**

### [1564] retry storm {#rune-1564}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Recovery logic amplifying failure instead of containing it. Unguided retries can turn a partial outage into a self-made siege.

**Shadow:** when retries synchronize into an outage amplifier rather than a recovery path.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `retry storm` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Recovery logic amplifying failure instead of containing it. Unguided retries can turn a partial outage into a self-made siege.
- Ask the assistant to state the `retry storm` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `retry storm` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Recovery logic amplifying failure instead of containing it.

### [1567] schema drift {#rune-1567}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** The stored world and the assumed world have silently stopped matching. This is how migrations keep hurting after everyone thinks they are done.

**Shadow:** when schema changes are shipped without compatibility checks, migration windows, or readers.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `schema drift` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: The stored world and the assumed world have silently stopped matching. This is how migrations keep hurting after everyone thinks they are done.
- Ask the assistant to state the `schema drift` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `schema drift` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Stored shape and assumed shape have silently diverged.

### [1568] split-brain {Curse} {#rune-1568}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Semantic status:** reviewed

**Force:** Separated leaders each believe they are authoritative.

**Shadow:** misusing `split-brain` at rune 1568 in the Curse lane can let early naming can freeze investigation around a symptom; verify by requiring the caster to state the evidence, rejected alternatives, and repair boundary.

**Prompt uses:**

- Name `split-brain` when the prompt needs this obligation made explicit: Separated leaders each believe they are authoritative.
- Ask the assistant to state the `split-brain` shadow before proposing changes.

**Example clause:**

Before editing, state the `split-brain` obligation, the evidence that satisfies it, and the failure mode if it is missing.

**Pocket gloss:** Separated leaders each believe they are authoritative.
